Two Linked Rings

Source: R/misread-tsne.R
link_data.Rd

Points sampled from two linked circles, from "How to Use t-SNE Effectively".

Usage

link_data(n)

Arguments

n

Number of points to sample.

Value

Data frame with coordinates in the x, y, z columns, and color in the color column.

Details

Creates a 3D dataset where points are uniformly sampled from two (2D) circles of unit radius, separated by 1 unit, and hence are overlapping. The two circles are not co-planar. Points are colored depending from which circle they are sampled from.

For the unlinked version, see unlink_data.

References

http://distill.pub/2016/misread-tsne/

See also

Other distill functions: circle_data(), cube_data(), gaussian_data(), grid_data(), long_cluster_data(), long_gaussian_data(), ortho_curve(), random_circle_cluster_data(), random_circle_data(), random_jump(), random_walk(), simplex_data(), subset_clusters_data(), three_clusters_data(), trefoil_data(), two_clusters_data(), two_different_clusters_data(), unlink_data()

Examples

df <- link_data(n = 50)
